advantages: Makes assumptions explicit via the prior instead of hiding them in ad hoc regularization.
Coherent uncertainty quantification: posteriors and credible intervals reflect both data and prior strength.
Natural handling of sequential learning—yesterday's posterior becomes today's prior as data arrive.
Unified view linking MLE, MAP and penalized regression through specific priors.
disadvantages: Results can be sensitive to prior choice when data are limited—requires sensitivity analysis.
Choosing 'objective' flat priors is subtle; improper priors may be improper or improper depending on reparameterization.
Exact posteriors are unavailable for most complex models—approximations (MCMC, VI) add engineering cost.
Mis-specified priors or likelihoods can overconfidence wrong conclusions if not checked.
